# Repository Naming Standards

## Prefixes by Product/Scope

| Prefix | Scope | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| `hyperi-*` | Company-wide, product-agnostic | `hyperi-rustlib`, `hyperi-pylib`, `hyperi-ai` |
| `dfe-*` | DFE product | `dfe-ingest`, `dfe-api`, `dfe-developer` |
| `edge-*` | Edge Stream Hub | `edge-collector`, `edge-gateway` |

- `hyperi-*` for shared libs, internal tooling, cross-product repos
- `dfe-*` for DFE-specific repos
- `edge-*` for Edge Stream Hub repos
- Repo spans two products → use `hyperi-*`

## External / Fork Repositories

- Retain upstream name for forks (e.g. `ClickHouse` stays `ClickHouse`)

## General Rules

- Lowercase, hyphen-separated. No underscores, no camelCase.
- 1–3 words after prefix. Descriptive but concise.
- Avoid redundant suffixes: `-service`, `-app`, `-repo`.

## Examples

| ❌ Don't | ✅ Do | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `my-service` | `dfe-ingest` | Must have product prefix |
| `dfe_ingest` | `dfe-ingest` | Hyphens, not underscores |
| `DFE-Ingest` | `dfe-ingest` | Lowercase only |
| `dfe-ingest-service` | `dfe-ingest` | Drop redundant `-service` |
| `renamed-clickhouse` | `ClickHouse` | Keep upstream name for forks |
